name: Indonesia
summary: Indonesia is a Southeast Asian mixed-legal-system unitary republic operating a dual personal-status framework with Muslim matters heard by Religious Courts (Pengadilan Agama) under the Compilation of Islamic Law (Kompilasi Hukum Islam 1991) and the Marriage Law 1/1974, and non-Muslim matters heard by the General Courts (Pengadilan Negeri) under civil-code principles inherited from the Dutch colonial period. Parental rights and custody are governed by Marriage Law 1/1974 arts. 41-49 (general) and KHI arts. 105-112 (Muslim matters). The Mahkamah Agung (Supreme Court of Indonesia, Jakarta) is the apex court for civil, criminal, religious-court and administrative matters; the Mahkamah Konstitusi (Constitutional Court) operates separate constitutional-review jurisdiction. Psychology profession is regulated under Law 23/2022 (On the Profession of Psychologist) establishing the Himpunan Psikologi Indonesia (HIMPSI) as the statutory professional-order regulator alongside the Ministry of Education licensing of the Psikolog title. Indonesia is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the kepentingan terbaik anak (best-interests-of-the-child) standard. Indonesia is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.title: Marriage Law 1974 — Parental responsibilities
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute governing marriage and parental responsibilities. Substantially amended by Law 16/2019 (raising minimum marriage age to 19). Arts. 41-49 govern parental rights and obligations including custody on dissolution of marriage.

statutory_framework.title: Compilation of Islamic Law — Custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal compilation of Islamic personal-law norms in force for Muslims via Religious Courts. Arts. 105-112 govern hadana (custody) provisions on Shafi'i jurisprudential basis.

statutory_framework.title: Law on the Profession of Psychologist
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute establishing the statutory framework for the psychology profession. Statutory title protection and HIMPSI professional-order regulation.

apex_courts.name: Mahkamah Agung (Supreme Court of Indonesia)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil, criminal, religious-court and administrative matters across both general and religious court systems.

apex_courts.name: Mahkamah Konstitusi (Constitutional Court)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Court with original jurisdiction over constitutional review of statutes.

professional_regulators.name: Himpunan Psikologi Indonesia (HIMPSI)
professional_regulators.role: Statutory professional-order regulator under Law 23/2022. Operates ethics code, disciplinary jurisdiction, and statutory licensing framework for psychologists.

anonymisation_convention: Indonesian family-court decisions are anonymised per Mahkamah Agung practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Indonesia independence + Sukarno-Hatta + Constitution-1945 framework
key_developments.description: Indonesia declared independence 17 August 1945 from Japanese occupation. Sukarno first president 1945-1967 + Mohammad Hatta first vice-president. Constitution of Indonesia 1945 (UUD 1945) adopted 18 August 1945 establishing presidential republic framework. Foundational constitutional anchor persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: 1965 Indonesian mass killings + Suharto-New-Order framework + 32-year-authoritarianism
key_developments.description: 1965 Indonesian mass killings 1 October 1965-March 1966 estimated 500,000-1,000,000 killed (PKI Communist Party members and alleged sympathisers). Subsequent Suharto New-Order regime 1967-1998 substantively distinctive 32-year-authoritarian framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Marriage Law 1/1974
key_developments.description: Federal Marriage Law enacted 2 January 1974 governing marriage and parental responsibilities. Arts. 41-49 govern parental rights and obligations including custody on dissolution of marriage. Foundational substantive-statutory framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Indonesia ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Indonesia ratified the UNCRC on 5 September 1990 (with reservations) — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 2002 Child Protection Law substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: Kompilasi Hukum Islam 1991
key_developments.description: Compilation of Islamic Law promulgated 10 June 1991 (Presidential Instruction 1/1991) for use in Religious Courts (Pengadilan Agama). Arts. 105-112 govern hadana (custody) provisions on Shafi'i jurisprudential basis. Foundational dual-personal-status framework substrate.

key_developments.title: Reformasi + Suharto-fall + democratic-transition framework
key_developments.description: 1998 Indonesian protests May 1998 substantively ending Suharto 32-year-authoritarian framework — Reformasi (Reformation) era. Substantively distinctive globally Indonesian democratic-transition framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory including subsequent 1999-2002 constitutional-amendments.

key_developments.title: East Timor independence-referendum + Aceh-conflict context
key_developments.description: East Timor independence-referendum 30 August 1999 — 78.5% in favour of independence. Subsequent independence 2002. Substantively distinctive globally Indonesian-secession framework. Aceh-conflict 1976-2005 ending Helsinki MoU 15 August 2005 substantively significant Indonesian armed-conflict framework affecting demographic and family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Child Protection Law 23/2002 + UNCRC-aligned framework
key_developments.description: Child Protection Law 23/2002 enacted 22 October 2002 — substantively significant child-protection-framework consolidation aligned with UNCRC obligations. Subsequent Law 35/2014 amendments substantively distinctive child-welfare-modernisation framework.

key_developments.title: Constitutional Court Decision 46/PUU-VIII/2010 + non-marital child rights framework
key_developments.description: Constitutional Court of Indonesia (Mahkamah Konstitusi) Decision 46/PUU-VIII/2010 of 17 February 2012 — substantively distinctive Indonesian non-marital-child-rights framework. Substantive MK 46/PUU-VIII/2010 framework ruling that non-marital children have civil-relationship + inheritance rights with biological father (anak luar kawin) if biological paternity proven through DNA + technology + science framework, modifying Marriage Law 1/1974 Article 43(1) — landmark constitutional ruling on child-paternity-rights framework. Foundation for subsequent 2014 Constitutional Court 13/PUU-XV/2014 + 2019 Law 16/2019 frameworks + Indonesia Supreme Court welfare-of-the-child substantive register integrating MK 46/PUU-VIII/2010 doctrine.

key_developments.title: Law 16/2019 amendment
key_developments.description: Marriage Law amended to raise minimum marriage age to 19 for both spouses 14 October 2019. Substantively significant child-marriage-prevention framework. Substantive Reformasi-era family-law-modernisation framework.

key_developments.title: KUHP Law 1/2023 New Criminal Code + cohabitation-criminalisation + family-law-intersection framework
key_developments.description: New Indonesian Criminal Code (Kitab Undang-Undang Hukum Pidana / KUHP) Law 1/2023 enacted 2 January 2023 effective 2 January 2026 replacing Dutch-colonial Wetboek van Strafrecht 1915 — substantively distinctive globally first major post-Independence Indonesian criminal-code reform framework. Articles 411-413 criminalising cohabitation (kumpul kebo) + extramarital sex + adultery framework + Articles 414-416 family-related criminal frameworks + structurally distinctive globally only modern G20 jurisdiction enacting formal cohabitation-criminalisation criminal-code framework in modern era + substantive family-law-intersection framework affecting subsequent family-court adjudication. Foundational pre-2026-implementation framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Law 23/2022 Psychologist Profession + Prabowo presidency context
key_developments.description: Statutory framework for psychology profession with HIMPSI as statutory regulator Law 23/2022 effective 24 March 2022. Prabowo Subianto inaugurated 20 October 2024 substantively distinctive Reformasi-era political-institutional framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and family-law-implementation.

structural_findings: Indonesia operates a structurally distinctive dual personal-status framework — Religious Courts (Pengadilan Agama) for Muslims under KHI + Marriage Law + Civil Code (Dutch-inherited) for non-Muslims via General Courts. Distinctive within the corpus for the parallel-court structure with shared apex appellate jurisdiction at Mahkamah Agung.
HIMPSI statutory professional-order regime (Law 23/2022) places Indonesia among the federal-statutory psychology regulator group within the corpus.
Non-Hague Convention status places Indonesia in the non-Hague Asian cluster. Relevant to cross-border-displacement custody matters.
editorial_notes: Indonesia jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 4 to 10 key_developments with full Sukarno-to-Prabowo trajectory: 1945-Indonesia-independence-+-Sukarno-Hatta-+-Constitution-1945 + 1965-1965-Indonesian-mass-killings-+-Suharto-New-Order-+-32-year-authoritarianism + 1974-Marriage-Law-1-1974 + 1990-UNCRC-ratification + 1991-Kompilasi-Hukum-Islam + 1998-Reformasi-+-Suharto-fall-+-democratic-transition + 1999-East-Timor-independence-referendum-+-Aceh-conflict + 2002-Child-Protection-Law-23-2002 + 2019-Law-16-2019-amendment-+-19-marriage-age + 2024-Law-23-2022-Psychologist-Profession-+-Prabowo-presidency.
Dual personal-status framework (Religious Courts Muslim + General Courts non-Muslim). Marriage Law 1/1974 + KHI 1991 + Law 23/2022 HIMPSI psychology regulator + non-Hague Convention.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive kepentingan-terbaik-anak analysis under Marriage Law 1/1974 + KHI 1991 + Child Protection Law 23/2002 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Asian + Southeast-Asian + dual-personal-status-Religious-Courts-Pengadilan-Agama-Muslim-+-General-Courts-Pengadilan-Negeri-non-Muslim + federal-statutory-psychology-regulator-HIMPSI-Law-23-2022 + Sukarno-Hatta-founding-leadership-1945 + 1965-Indonesian-mass-killings-500000-to-1-million-killed + Suharto-32-year-New-Order-1967-1998 + Reformasi-1998-democratic-transition + East-Timor-independence-referendum-1999-78.5-percent + Aceh-conflict-1976-2005-+-Helsinki-MoU + Marriage-Law-1-1974 + Compilation-of-Islamic-Law-KHI-1991-Shafi'i + Child-Protection-Law-23-2002 + Prabowo-presidency-2024 + non-Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus.
